title_candidates: Claude Opus 4.7 lands — what's actually different for everyday users
A clearer Claude: what changed in Opus 4.7
Opus 4.7 is out — five minutes to try it
summary: Anthropic shipped Claude Opus 4.7 as its newest flagship model. Here's what changed for normal users — and how to try it in five minutes without writing a line of code.

primary_source.title: Claude Opus 4.7 — release notes
primary_source.author: Anthropic
key_facts: Anthropic released Claude Opus 4.7, its newest flagship model in the Opus 4 line.
The release is positioned around improvements on long, multi-step tasks: planning, long-form writing, and tool use over extended sessions.
Anthropic highlights better instruction-following stability across long conversations.
Users on Claude.ai can pick Opus 4.7 from the model menu — no setup required.
A 'Fast' option streams output more aggressively but uses the same underlying model.

quote_candidates.text: improvements in instruction-following stability and tool use over very long sessions
quote_candidates.position: Anthropic release notes (paraphrased — kept for shape reference)

steps.instruction: Open claude.ai and sign in (or sign up — it's free).
In the model picker at the top of a new chat, choose Opus 4.7.
Optional: turn on Fast in the chat options to stream replies more aggressively.
Paste a real task you've stalled on (e.g., 'Plan a 7-day Tokyo trip for a family with a stroller and a $3,000 food budget'). Ask for a checklist.
Reply with the part you actually want changed (e.g., 'Cut anything that requires walking more than 2 km a day') to test how it carries earlier instructions forward.
